title: Emma Raducanu's New Coaching Plan
summary: Emma Raducanu is a famous tennis player from Britain who won a big tournament called the US Open in 2021. She is ranked 24th in the world, which is her best ranking since 2022. Right now, she is playing at Indian Wells in California. Emma wants to get back to playing tennis in her natural way because she feels that too many coaches have told her how to play, and it didn't always work for her. She is not sure about having one full-time coach because she worries they will be watched too closely by others, and she might feel pressured to stay with them even if it's not right. Instead, she plans to 'tap into a few people' for help, which means she will ask different coaches for advice without making them her main coach. Mark Petchey is one person she is working with, and he helps her when he can. Emma hopes this new approach will help her play better and feel more confident on the court.

a person who teaches and helps athletes improve their skills
playing in a style that feels easy and right for you

background_read: Emma Raducanu is a young British tennis player who became famous by winning the US Open in 2021. In tennis, players often work with coaches to improve their game, but Emma has had many coaches and now wants to try a different approach. She is currently playing at Indian Wells, a big tennis event in California, and is focusing on playing in her own natural style instead of following strict instructions from coaches.
Article_Structure: WHO: Emma Raducanu, a British tennis player and 2021 US Open champion
WHAT: She is trying to rediscover her natural playing style by tapping into advice from multiple people instead of having one full-time coach
WHERE: At Indian Wells in California, where she is competing in a tennis tournament
WHY: Because she feels previous coaches dictated her play too much, and she wants to avoid pressure and scrutiny while finding a better fit for her game

perspectives.description: Emma wants to trust her instincts and avoid too much coaching interference to play better.
